outputs.pipeline_request_packet: Synthetic calibration request packet for an SE/LLM-wiki wikiization request. The packet binds the request to approved_source_policy and source_scope_binding, preserves query-first handling, and separates reusable existing projection use from any required fresh sourcebound stage.
outputs.routing_decision_note: Route as a composite party_sequence. Reuse the bounded existing projection for the query_only_with_existing_projection portion. Invoke fresh sourcebound work only for the stage whose requested scope is not covered by the existing projection. Treat archive/storage notes as handoff metadata only, not source or canon authority.
outputs.stage_chain_manifest: Stage chain: request intake and routing decision; existing projection eligibility check; fresh sourcebound projection for uncovered scope; archive/package handoff note; Obsidian export decision; metadata and knowledge access capture; closeout review. Stronger gates are inserted only for the fresh sourcebound stage and the required closeout review.
outputs.source_packet_refs: Synthetic public-safe refs: approved_source_policy_ref, source_scope_binding_ref, request_packet_ref. These refs identify bounded policy and scope anchors only and do not expose raw source payloads or private evidence.
outputs.sourcebound_packet_refs: Synthetic public-safe refs: existing_projection_ref for the reusable bounded projection; fresh_sourcebound_stage_ref for the uncovered scope requiring new sourcebound work. Neither ref grants independent source authority beyond the approved request scope.
outputs.archive_manifest_refs: Synthetic public-safe refs: pre_intake_owner_archive_ref and post_projection_package_backup_ref. These refs record storage or handoff status only; they do not promote canon, source authority, or workflow readiness.
outputs.obsidian_export_refs: Synthetic public-safe ref: generated_read_only_obsidian_view_ref. The export is a local read-only view over canon-backed knowledge only and excludes raw source files, private payloads, and NotebookLM answers.
outputs.knowledge_access_refs: Synthetic public-safe refs: metadata_capture_ref and knowledge_access_event_ref. These capture access/routing metadata for the bounded request without disclosing private/raw evidence.
outputs.closeout_review_refs: Synthetic public-safe ref: required_closeout_review_ref. The closeout confirms boundary preservation, sourcebound handling, archive non-authority, and absence of upstream mutation claims.
outputs.boundary_review_note: Public-safe boundary review: no tool use, file edits, runtime paths, hidden refs, private/raw evidence, upstream mutation, or source/canon authority promotion is claimed.

provenance: Derived only from the supplied public-safe synthetic workflow contract.
Uses the stated synthetic scenario facts: one reusable projection, one fresh sourcebound stage, one non-authoritative archive note, and one required closeout review ref.
gaps: No real source packet contents, runtime evidence, private projection payloads, or archive locations are available or asserted.
Calibration cannot validate actual execution success, file state, or upstream registration state beyond the synthetic contract statements.
downstream_handoff: Hand off the reusable existing projection ref only for the bounded query_only_with_existing_projection scope.
Hand off the fresh_sourcebound_stage_ref, archive manifest refs, Obsidian export ref, knowledge access ref, and closeout review ref as synthetic comparison artifacts, not as canon promotion evidence.
boundary_review_note: Archive/storage refs remain non-authoritative and do not promote canon or source authority.
Obsidian export remains a generated read-only local view and must not expose private/raw payloads, NotebookLM answers, or hidden evidence.
no_claims: No claim of tool use, file edits, runtime paths, hidden private evidence, or upstream artifact mutation.
No claim that archive storage, Obsidian export, or synthetic refs grant stronger source authority, canon ownership, or readiness than pilot-executed.
